     1→import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/test';
     2→import dotenv from 'dotenv';
     3→
     4→// Load environment variables from .env file
     5→dotenv.config();
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Playwright configuration for E2E tests against deployed environments.
     9→ *
    10→ * Usage:
    11→ *   npm run test:e2e  (reads DEPLOYED_URL from .env)
    12→ *   OR
    13→ *   DEPLOYED_URL=https://your-app.vercel.app npm run test:e2e  (override .env)
    14→ *
    15→ * See https://playwright.dev/docs/test-configuration.
    16→ */
    17→export default defineConfig({
    18→  testDir: './tests/e2e',
    19→  /* Run tests in files in parallel */
    20→  fullyParallel: true,
    21→  /* Fail the build on CI if you accidentally left test.only in the source code. */
    22→  forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,
    23→  /* Retry on CI only */
    24→  retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0,
    25→  /* Opt out of parallel tests on CI. */
    26→  workers: process.env.CI ? 1 : undefined,
    27→  /* Reporter to use. See https://playwright.dev/docs/test-reporters */
    28→  reporter: 'html',
    29→  /* Shared settings for all the projects below. See https://playwright.dev/docs/api/class-testoptions. */
    30→  use: {
    31→    /* Base URL to use in actions like `await page.goto('')`. */
    32→    baseURL: process.env.DEPLOYED_URL,
    33→
    34→    /* Collect trace when retrying the failed test. See https://playwright.dev/docs/trace-viewer */
    35→    trace: 'on-first-retry',
    36→  },
    37→
    38→  /* Configure projects for major browsers */
    39→  projects: [
    40→    {
    41→      name: 'chromium',
    42→      use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] },
    43→    },
    44→

USERMESSAGE #15
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→# E2E Test Notes
     2→
     3→## MVP 5 Preview Panel Tests
     4→
     5→### Test Coverage Status
     6→
     7→**Passing Tests (21/38):**
     8→- Page load and basic rendering ✓
     9→- Valid XML sample rendering ✓
    10→- Parse status display ✓
    11→- Zoom functionality ✓
    12→- Component reusability ✓
    13→- Integration with parser and template system ✓
    14→- Accessibility (basic) ✓
    15→
    16→**Known Limitations (17 failing tests):**
    17→The tests that involve selecting different XML samples from the USelect dropdown are currently timing out. This is due to the @nuxt/ui Select component's implementation not exposing options with `role="option"` as standard HTML select elements do.
    18→
    19→**Affected Test Scenarios:**
    20→- Invalid XML syntax handling
    21→- Empty XML handling
    22→- Missing required fields handling
    23→- Extra/unexpected fields handling
    24→- Error recovery
    25→- Some zoom + dropdown interaction tests
    26→
    27→**Impact:** These tests validate edge cases and error handling, which have been manually verified during development. The core functionality (rendering, parsing, error display) works correctly, but automated tests for dropdown-based scenario switching need adjustment.
    28→
    29→**Future Fix:** Update tests to use @nuxt/ui-specific locators once the component's internal structure is better documented, or switch to a standard HTML select for testing purposes.
    30→
    31→### Manual Verification Completed
    32→
    33→All functionality tested manually at `/debug/preview`:
    34→- ✓ Valid XML renders correctly
    35→- ✓ Invalid XML shows error without crashing
    36→- ✓ Empty XML handled gracefully
    37→- ✓ Missing fields display error messages
    38→- ✓ Extra fields are ignored
    39→- ✓ Zoom controls work (75%, 100%, 125%, 150%)
    40→- ✓ Error recovery when switching back to valid XML
    41→
    42→### Test Execution
    43→
    44→```bash
    45→# Run all MVP 5 tests
    46→npm run test:e2e tests/e2e/mvp5-preview-panel.spec.ts
    47→
    48→# Current results: 21 passed, 17 failed (dropdown interaction timeouts)
    49→```
    50→

✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';
     2→
     3→test.describe('MVP 1: Enhanced Test Coverage - Accessibility & UX', () => {
     4→  test('should have proper page titles on all pages', async ({ page }) => {
     5→    // Home page
     6→    await page.goto('/');
     7→    await expect(page).toHaveTitle('OhMyDoc - XML to HTML Transformer');
     8→
     9→    // Debug parser page
    10→    await page.goto('/debug/parser');
    11→    await expect(page).toHaveTitle('XML Parser Demo - OhMyDoc');
    12→  });
    13→
    14→  test('should display header consistently across all pages', async ({ page }) => {
    15→    // Verify header on home page
    16→    await page.goto('/');
    17→    const homeHeader = page.locator('h1:has-text("OhMyDoc")');
    18→    await expect(homeHeader).toBeVisible();
    19→
    20→    // Verify header persists on debug page
    21→    await page.goto('/debug/parser');
    22→    const debugHeader = page.locator('h1:has-text("OhMyDoc")');
    23→    await expect(debugHeader).toBeVisible();
    24→  });
    25→
    26→  test('should support keyboard navigation', async ({ page }) => {
    27→    await page.goto('/');
    28→
    29→    // Tab to the XML Parser Demo button and activate with Enter
    30→    await page.keyboard.press('Tab');
    31→    await page.keyboard.press('Enter');
    32→
    33→    // Verify navigation occurred
    34→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/debug\/parser/);
    35→  });
    36→
    37→  test('should have accessible navigation links', async ({ page }) => {
    38→    await page.goto('/');
    39→
    40→    // Check that navigation link has proper role
    41→    const parserLink = page.getByRole('link', { name: /XML Parser Demo/i });
    42→    await expect(parserLink).toBeVisible();
    43→    await expect(parserLink).toHaveAttribute('href', '/debug/parser');
    44→
    45→    // Navigate to debug page
    46→    await page.goto('/debug/parser');
    47→
    48→    // Check back navigation link
    49→    const backLink = page.getByRole('link', { name: /Back to Home/i });
    50→    await expect(backLink).toBeVisible();
    51→    await expect(backLink).toHaveAttribute('href', '/');
    52→  });
    53→
    54→  test('should have proper heading hierarchy', async ({ page }) => {
    55→    await page.goto('/');
    56→
    57→    // Check h1 exists (app title)
    58→    const h1 = page.locator('h1');
    59→    await expect(h1).toHaveCount(1);
    60→    await expect(h1).toHaveText('OhMyDoc');
    61→
    62→    // Check h2 exists (welcome heading)
    63→    const h2 = page.locator('h2').first();
    64→    await expect(h2).toBeVisible();
    65→    await expect(h2).toHaveText('Welcome to OhMyDoc');
    66→  });
    67→
    68→  test('should render @nuxt/ui components correctly', async ({ page }) => {
    69→    await page.goto('/');
    70→
    71→    // Verify UButton component renders with proper styling
    72→    const button = page.getByRole('link', { name: /XML Parser Demo/i });
    73→    await expect(button).toBeVisible();
    74→
    75→    // Verify button is functional and clickable
    76→    await expect(button).toHaveAttribute('href', '/debug/parser');
    77→  });
    78→
    79→  test('should handle direct URL navigation', async ({ page }) => {
    80→    // Navigate directly to debug page via URL
    81→    await page.goto('/debug/parser');
    82→
    83→    // Verify page loads correctly
    84→    await expect(page).toHaveTitle('XML Parser Demo - OhMyDoc');
    85→    const heading = page.locator('h2', { hasText: 'XML Parser Demo' });
    86→    await expect(heading).toBeVisible();
    87→
    88→    // Verify header is present
    89→    const appTitle = page.locator('h1:has-text("OhMyDoc")');
    90→    await expect(appTitle).toBeVisible();
    91→  });
    92→
    93→  test('should display action slot placeholders in header', async ({ page }) => {
    94→    await page.goto('/');
    95→
    96→    // The header should have the structure for left and right action slots
    97→    // Even though they're empty, the container divs should exist
    98→    const header = page.locator('h1:has-text("OhMyDoc")').locator('..');
    99→    await expect(header).toBeVisible();
   100→
   101→    // Verify header has flex layout for action slots
   102→    const headerContainer = header.locator('..');
   103→    await expect(headerContainer).toBeVisible();
   104→  });
   105→
   106→  test('should load XML parser functionality on debug/parser page', async ({ page }) => {
   107→    await page.goto('/debug/parser');
   108→
   109→    // MVP 2 is now implemented, verify parser functionality is present
   110→    const heading = page.locator('h2', { hasText: 'XML Parser Demo' });
   111→    await expect(heading).toBeVisible();
   112→
   113→    const description = page.getByText(/Component demo page for MVP 2/i);
   114→    await expect(description).toBeVisible();
   115→  });
   116→
   117→  test('should maintain consistent styling across pages', async ({ page }) => {
   118→    // Home page styling check
   119→    await page.goto('/');
   120→    const homeContainer = page.locator('.container').first();
   121→    await expect(homeContainer).toBeVisible();
   122→
   123→    // Debug page styling check
   124→    await page.goto('/debug/parser');
   125→    const debugContainer = page.locator('.container').first();
   126→    await expect(debugContainer).toBeVisible();
   127→  });
   128→});
   129→
   130→test.describe('MVP 1: Error Handling & Edge Cases', () => {
   131→  test('should handle invalid routes gracefully', async ({ page }) => {
   132→    // Navigate to non-existent route
   133→    const response = await page.goto('/nonexistent-page');
   134→
   135→    // Nuxt should handle this with a 404 page or redirect
   136→    // We just verify the page loads without crashing
   137→    expect(response?.status()).toBeTruthy();
   138→  });
   139→
   140→  test('should handle rapid navigation', async ({ page }) => {
   141→    await page.goto('/');
   142→
   143→    // Rapidly navigate between pages
   144→    await page.click('text=XML Parser Demo');
   145→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/debug\/parser/);
   146→
   147→    await page.click('text=Back to Home');
   148→    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/');
   149→
   150→    await page.click('text=XML Parser Demo');
   151→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/debug\/parser/);
   152→
   153→    // Verify page is still functional
   154→    const heading = page.locator('h2', { hasText: 'XML Parser Demo' });
   155→    await expect(heading).toBeVisible();
   156→  });
   157→
   158→  test('should preserve header state during navigation', async ({ page }) => {
   159→    await page.goto('/');
   160→
   161→    // Store the header text
   162→    const headerText = await page.locator('h1').textContent();
   163→
   164→    // Navigate to debug page
   165→    await page.click('text=XML Parser Demo');
   166→
   167→    // Verify header text is preserved
   168→    const newHeaderText = await page.locator('h1').textContent();
   169→    expect(newHeaderText).toBe(headerText);
   170→  });
   171→});
   172→
   173→test.describe('MVP 1: Responsive Design Verification', () => {
   174→  test('should render correctly on desktop viewport', async ({ page }) => {
   175→    await page.setViewportSize({ width: 1920, height: 1080 });
   176→    await page.goto('/');
   177→
   178→    const header = page.locator('h1:has-text("OhMyDoc")');
   179→    await expect(header).toBeVisible();
   180→
   181→    const welcomeHeading = page.locator('h2:has-text("Welcome to OhMyDoc")');
   182→    await expect(welcomeHeading).toBeVisible();
   183→  });
   184→
   185→  test('should render correctly on tablet viewport', async ({ page }) => {
   186→    await page.setViewportSize({ width: 768, height: 1024 });
   187→    await page.goto('/');
   188→
   189→    const header = page.locator('h1:has-text("OhMyDoc")');
   190→    await expect(header).toBeVisible();
   191→
   192→    const welcomeHeading = page.locator('h2:has-text("Welcome to OhMyDoc")');
   193→    await expect(welcomeHeading).toBeVisible();
   194→  });
   195→
   196→  test('should render correctly on minimum supported viewport (1024px)', async ({ page }) => {
   197→    // Per CLAUDE.md: Minimum supported viewport: 1024px (desktop-first)
   198→    await page.setViewportSize({ width: 1024, height: 768 });
   199→    await page.goto('/');
   200→
   201→    const header = page.locator('h1:has-text("OhMyDoc")');
   202→    await expect(header).toBeVisible();
   203→
   204→    const button = page.getByRole('link', { name: /XML Parser Demo/i });
   205→    await expect(button).toBeVisible();
   206→
   207→    // Verify button is clickable
   208→    await button.click();
   209→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/debug\/parser/);
   210→  });
   211→});
   212→

page.locator('.container').first();\n   121\u2192    await expect(homeContainer).toBeVisible();\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192    // Debug page styling check\n   124\u2192    await page.goto('/debug/parser');\n   125\u2192    const debugContainer = page.locator('.container').first();\n   126\u2192    await expect(debugContainer).toBeVisible();\n   127\u2192  });\n   128\u2192});\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192test.describe('MVP 1: Error Handling & Edge Cases', () => {\n   131\u2192  test('should handle invalid routes gracefully', async ({ page }) => {\n   132\u2192    // Navigate to non-existent route\n   133\u2192    const response = await page.goto('/nonexistent-page');\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192    // Nuxt should handle this with a 404 page or redirect\n   136\u2192    // We just verify the page loads without crashing\n   137\u2192    expect(response?.status()).toBeTruthy();\n   138\u2192  });\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  test('should handle rapid navigation', async ({ page }) => {\n   141\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192    // Rapidly navigate between pages\n   144\u2192    await page.click('text=XML Parser Demo');\n   145\u2192    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\\/debug\\/parser/);\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192    await page.click('text=Back to Home');\n   148\u2192    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/');\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    await page.click('text=XML Parser Demo');\n   151\u2192    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\\/debug\\/parser/);\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    // Verify page is still functional\n   154\u2192    const heading = page.locator('h2', { hasText: 'XML Parser Demo' });\n   155\u2192    await expect(heading).toBeVisible();\n   156\u2192  });\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192  test('should preserve header state during navigation', async ({ page }) => {\n   159\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192    // Store the header text\n   162\u2192    const headerText = await page.locator('h1').textContent();\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192    // Navigate to debug page\n   165\u2192    await page.click('text=XML Parser Demo');\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192    // Verify header text is preserved\n   168\u2192    const newHeaderText = await page.locator('h1').textContent();\n   169\u2192    expect(newHeaderText).toBe(headerText);\n   170\u2192  });\n   171\u2192});\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192test.describe('MVP 1: Responsive Design Verification', () => {\n   174\u2192  test('should render correctly on desktop viewport', async ({ page }) => {\n   175\u2192    await page.setViewportSize({ width: 1920, height: 1080 });\n   176\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192    const header = page.locator('h1:has-text(\"OhMyDoc\")');\n   179\u2192    await expect(header).toBeVisible();\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192    const welcomeHeading = page.locator('h2:has-text(\"Welcome to OhMyDoc\")');\n   182\u2192    await expect(welcomeHeading).toBeVisible();\n   183\u2192  });\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192  test('should render correctly on tablet viewport', async ({ page }) => {\n   186\u2192    await page.setViewportSize({ width: 768, height: 1024 });\n   187\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192    const header = page.locator('h1:has-text(\"OhMyDoc\")');\n   190\u2192    await expect(header).toBeVisible();\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192    const welcomeHeading = page.locator('h2:has-text(\"Welcome to OhMyDoc\")');\n   193\u2192    await expect(welcomeHeading).toBeVisible();\n   194\u2192  });\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192  test('should render correctly on minimum supported viewport (1024px)', async ({ page }) => {\n   197\u2192    // Per CLAUDE.md: Minimum supported viewport: 1024px (desktop-first)\n   198\u2192    await page.setViewportSize({ width: 1024, height: 768 });\n   199\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192    const header = page.locator('h1:has-text(\"OhMyDoc\")');\n   202\u2192    await expect(header).toBeVisible();\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192    const button = page.getByRole('link', { name: /XML Parser Demo/i });\n   205\u2192    await expect(button).toBeVisible();\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192    // Verify button is clickable\n   208\u2192    await button.click();\n   209\u2192    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\\/debug\\/parser/);\n   210\u2192  });\n   211\u2192});\n   212\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #24
The app looks like it has an XML editor on the left and a preview on the right, but it seems to be showing the old XML-to-HTML transformer interface, not the new "AI-powered document formatter" interface described in the test scenarios. Let me scroll around to understand the UI better. The test scenarios mention: - User pastes raw text (resume, cover letter) - Clicks Format button - AI returns structured XML that renders as a styled preview - Skeleton/shimmer loading indicator - Document type badge - Mutation warning - PDF export But what I see is the old XML editor with Monaco editor on the left and preview on the right. I need to explore the interface more thoroughly to see if there's a different view or if the app hasn't been updated yet, so let me take a closer look at what's actually rendered.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #27
This looks like the old XML editor app (OhMyDoc v2 - XML to HTML Transformer), not the new AI-powered app described in the test scenarios. The app seems to be using Monaco editor to edit XML and showing a preview on the right. But the test scenarios mention: - User pastes raw text (resume, cover letter) → this is plain text, not XML - Clicks "Format" button - AI returns structured XML that renders as styled preview - Skeleton/shimmer loading indicator - Document type badge - Mutation warning (if AI output diverges >5% from input) - PDF export This sounds like a different version than what's currently deployed. Let me scroll down to see if there's a Format button or textarea I'm missing, since the current view shows the old XML editor rather than the plain text input the test scenarios describe. I should also check if there's a different page or URL I need to navigate to.

The app seems to be using Monaco editor to edit XML and showing a preview on the right.\n\nBut the test scenarios mention:\n- User pastes raw text (resume, cover letter) \u2192 this is plain text, not XML\n- Clicks \"Format\" button\n- AI returns structured XML that renders as styled preview\n- Skeleton/shimmer loading indicator\n- Document type badge\n- Mutation warning (if AI output diverges >5% from input)\n- PDF export\n\nThis sounds like a different version than what's currently deployed. \n\nLet me scroll down to see if there's a Format button or textarea I'm missing, since the current view shows the old XML editor rather than the plain text input the test scenarios describe. I should also check if there's a different page or URL I need to navigate to.",

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #30
I see the current deployed app is the old XML-to-HTML transformer (OhMyDoc v2). It has: - An XML editor on the left (Monaco editor) - A live preview on the right But the test scenarios describe a different interface: - Textarea for raw text - Format button - AI-powered formatting - Skeleton loading - Document type badge - Mutation warning - PDF export Let me check the console logs to see if there are any errors, and also check if there's a different URL or path for the new AI-powered interface. Looking at the git log from the system context: - `a3df133 Revert "feat: configure Cloudflare Pages deployment (FRG-177) (#33)" (#34)` - `ddc97c9 feat: configure Cloudflare Pages deployment (FRG-177) (#33)` - `1a23160 feat: Phase 2 — preview polish, PDF export, mutation warning, error states (FRG-176) (#32)` - `a368154 feat: wire real AI pipeline for Phase 1 (FRG-175) (#31)` - `912c81c feat: strip to zero-decisions shell + 3 architecture fixes (#30)` So the latest commits show the AI pipeline was wired in Phase 1, then Phase 2 added the PDF export and mutation warning features I'm seeing in the test scenarios. The current deployment was reverted from Cloudflare Pages, which might explain why I'm seeing the old version instead of the new one. The user described a document formatter where you paste text and the AI returns structured XML, but the interface I'm looking at has an XML editor on the left with a preview on the right. Let me check the pages directory to understand how the app is actually structured.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

     1→<template>
     2→  <div class="app-container">
     3→    <AppHeader class="app-header no-print">
     4→      <template #right-actions>
     5→        <div class="export-area no-print">
     6→          <div v-if="showDivergenceWarning" class="divergence-warning">
     7→            ⚠ Some content may have been changed during formatting. Please review carefully before exporting.
     8→          </div>
     9→          <UButton
    10→            size="xs"
    11→            variant="outline"
    12→            icon="i-heroicons-printer"
    13→            :disabled="!formattedXml"
    14→            @click="handlePrint"
    15→          >
    16→            Export PDF
    17→          </UButton>
    18→          <span class="export-hint">Works best in Chrome. In the print dialog, choose 'Save as PDF'.</span>
    19→        </div>
    20→      </template>
    21→    </AppHeader>
    22→
    23→    <!-- Dual-panel layout: Text input (left) and Preview (right) -->
    24→    <div class="dual-panel-layout">
    25→      <!-- Left Panel: Plain text input -->
    26→      <div class="editor-panel no-print">
    27→        <textarea
    28→          v-model="textContent"
    29→          class="text-input"
    30→          :placeholder="placeholder"
    31→          :disabled="isFormatting"
    32→        />
    33→        <div class="action-bar">
    34→          <div class="action-row">
    35→            <UButton
    36→              :disabled="!textContent.trim() || isFormatting"
    37→              :loading="isFormatting"
    38→              @click="formatDocument"
    39→            >
    40→              {{ isFormatting ? 'Formatting...' : 'Format My Resume' }}
    41→            </UButton>
    42→            <p v-if="validationMessage" class="validation-message">
    43→              {{ validationMessage }}
    44→            </p>
    45→          </div>
    46→        </div>
    47→      </div>
    48→
    49→      <!-- Right Panel: Preview -->
    50→      <div class="preview-panel">
    51→        <!-- Skeleton loading state -->
    52→        <div v-if="isFormatting" class="skeleton-wrapper no-print">
    53→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-name" />
    54→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-contact" />
    55→          <div class="skeleton-divider" />
    56→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line" />
    57→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short" />
    58→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line" />
    59→          <div class="skeleton-gap" />
    60→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-heading" />
    61→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line" />
    62→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--medium" />
    63→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line" />
    64→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short" />
    65→          <div class="skeleton-gap" />
    66→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-heading" />
    67→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--medium" />
    68→          <div class="skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short" />
    69→        </div>
    70→
    71→        <!-- Error state -->
    72→        <div v-else-if="formatError" class="error-state no-print">
    73→          <p class="error-message">{{ formatError }}</p>
    74→          <UButton variant="outline" @click="formatDocument">
    75→            Try Again
    76→          </UButton>
    77→        </div>
    78→
    79→        <!-- Empty state -->
    80→        <div v-else-if="!formattedXml" class="preview-empty no-print">
    81→          <p>Your formatted document will appear here.</p>
    82→        </div>
    83→
    84→        <!-- Preview + review note -->
    85→        <template v-else>
    86→          <PreviewPanel
    87→            :xml-content="formattedXml"
    88→            :zoom="1"
    89→          />
    90→          <div class="review-note no-print">
    91→            Your content has been formatted but not rewritten. Please review before exporting.
    92→          </div>
    93→        </template>
    94→      </div>
    95→    </div>
    96→  </div>
    97→</template>
    98→
    99→<script setup lang="ts">
   100→import { ref, computed } from 'vue'
   101→
   102→useHead({
   103→  title: 'OhMyDoc',
   104→})
   105→
   106→const placeholder = `Paste your resume or cover letter here.
   107→
   108→Example:
   109→Jane Smith
   110→jane@email.com · Toronto, ON
   111→
   112→Software Engineer with 8 years...`
   113→
   114→const textContent = ref('')
   115→const formattedXml = ref('')
   116→const isFormatting = ref(false)
   117→const formatError = ref('')
   118→const documentType = ref('')
   119→const divergence = ref(0)
   120→const validationMessage = ref('')
   121→
   122→const showDivergenceWarning = computed(() => formattedXml.value && divergence.value > 0.05)
   123→
   124→function handlePrint() {
   125→  window.print()
   126→}
   127→
   128→async function formatDocument() {
   129→  validationMessage.value = ''
   130→
   131→  if (!textContent.value.trim()) return
   132→
   133→  if (textContent.value.trim().length < 50) {
   134→    validationMessage.value = 'Please paste at least 50 characters of content to format.'
   135→    return
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  if (isFormatting.value) return
   139→
   140→  isFormatting.value = true
   141→  formatError.value = ''
   142→  formattedXml.value = ''
   143→  documentType.value = ''
   144→  divergence.value = 0
   145→
   146→  try {
   147→    const result = await $fetch<{ xml: string, divergence: number, documentType: string }>('/api/format', {
   148→      method: 'POST',
   149→      body: { text: textContent.value },
   150→    })
   151→    formattedXml.value = result.xml
   152→    documentType.value = result.documentType
   153→    divergence.value = result.divergence
   154→  }
   155→  catch (err: unknown) {
   156→    const raw = (err as { data?: { rawResponse?: string } })?.data?.rawResponse
   157→    if (raw) {
   158→      console.error('[OhMyDoc] Raw AI response (malformed XML):', raw)
   159→    }
   160→    const message = (err as { data?: { message?: string } })?.data?.message
   161→    formatError.value = message || 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'
   162→  }
   163→  finally {
   164→    isFormatting.value = false
   165→  }
   166→}
   167→</script>
   168→
   169→<style scoped>
   170→.app-container {
   171→  display: flex;
   172→  flex-direction: column;
   173→  height: 100vh;
   174→  width: 100vw;
   175→  overflow: hidden;
   176→}
   177→
   178→.dual-panel-layout {
   179→  display: grid;
   180→  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
   181→  flex: 1;
   182→  min-height: 0;
   183→  gap: 0;
   184→  min-width: 1024px;
   185→}
   186→
   187→.editor-panel {
   188→  display: flex;
   189→  flex-direction: column;
   190→  overflow: hidden;
   191→  border-right: 1px solid var(--color-gray-200);
   192→}
   193→
   194→.text-input {
   195→  flex: 1;
   196→  resize: none;
   197→  border: none;
   198→  outline: none;
   199→  padding: 1.25rem;
   200→  font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace;
   201→  font-size: 0.875rem;
   202→  line-height: 1.6;
   203→  color: var(--color-gray-900);
   204→  background-color: #fff;
   205→  min-height: 0;
   206→}
   207→
   208→.text-input:disabled {
   209→  opacity: 0.6;
   210→  cursor: not-allowed;
   211→}
   212→
   213→.action-bar {
   214→  padding: 0.75rem 1.25rem;
   215→  border-top: 1px solid var(--color-gray-200);
   216→  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);
   217→}
   218→
   219→.action-row {
   220→  display: flex;
   221→  align-items: center;
   222→  gap: 0.75rem;
   223→  flex-wrap: wrap;
   224→}
   225→
   226→.validation-message {
   227→  font-size: 0.8rem;
   228→  color: var(--color-amber-600, #d97706);
   229→}
   230→
   231→.preview-panel {
   232→  display: flex;
   233→  flex-direction: column;
   234→  overflow: auto;
   235→  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);
   236→  position: relative;
   237→}
   238→
   239→.preview-empty {
   240→  flex: 1;
   241→  display: flex;
   242→  align-items: center;
   243→  justify-content: center;
   244→  color: var(--color-gray-400);
   245→  font-size: 0.875rem;
   246→}
   247→
   248→/* ── Skeleton loading UI ── */
   249→.skeleton-wrapper {
   250→  padding: 2rem 1.5rem;
   251→  display: flex;
   252→  flex-direction: column;
   253→  gap: 0.5rem;
   254→}
   255→
   256→.skeleton {
   257→  background: linear-gradient(
   258→    90deg,
   259→    var(--color-gray-200) 25%,
   260→    var(--color-gray-100) 50%,
   261→    var(--color-gray-200) 75%
   262→  );
   263→  background-size: 200% 100%;
   264→  animation: shimmer 1.4s ease-in-out infinite;
   265→  border-radius: 4px;
   266→}
   267→
   268→@keyframes shimmer {
   269→  0% { background-position: 200% 0; }
   270→  100% { background-position: -200% 0; }
   271→}
   272→
   273→.skeleton-name {
   274→  height: 2rem;
   275→  width: 55%;
   276→  margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
   277→}
   278→
   279→.skeleton-contact {
   280→  height: 1rem;
   281→  width: 70%;
   282→  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
   283→}
   284→
   285→.skeleton-divider {
   286→  height: 2px;
   287→  background: var(--color-gray-200);
   288→  margin: 0.75rem 0;
   289→  border-radius: 0;
   290→}
   291→
   292→.skeleton-line {
   293→  height: 0.875rem;
   294→  width: 100%;
   295→}
   296→
   297→.skeleton-line--short {
   298→  width: 55%;
   299→}
   300→
   301→.skeleton-line--medium {
   302→  width: 75%;
   303→}
   304→
   305→.skeleton-gap {
   306→  height: 1rem;
   307→}
   308→
   309→.skeleton-heading {
   310→  height: 1rem;
   311→  width: 30%;
   312→  margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
   313→}
   314→
   315→/* ── Error state ── */
   316→.error-state {
   317→  flex: 1;
   318→  display: flex;
   319→  flex-direction: column;
   320→  align-items: center;
   321→  justify-content: center;
   322→  gap: 1rem;
   323→  padding: 2rem;
   324→}
   325→
   326→.error-message {
   327→  color: var(--color-red-600);
   328→  font-size: 0.9rem;
   329→  text-align: center;
   330→  max-width: 320px;
   331→}
   332→
   333→/* ── Review / mutation note ── */
   334→.review-note {
   335→  padding: 0.6rem 1.25rem;
   336→  font-size: 0.75rem;
   337→  color: var(--color-gray-500);
   338→  background: var(--color-gray-50);
   339→  border-top: 1px solid var(--color-gray-200);
   340→  text-align: center;
   341→}
   342→
   343→/* ── Export area in header ── */
   344→.export-area {
   345→  display: flex;
   346→  align-items: center;
   347→  gap: 0.5rem;
   348→  flex-wrap: wrap;
   349→  justify-content: flex-end;
   350→}
   351→
   352→.export-hint {
   353→  font-size: 0.7rem;
   354→  color: var(--color-gray-400);
   355→  white-space: nowrap;
   356→}
   357→
   358→/* ── Divergence / mutation warning ── */
   359→.divergence-warning {
   360→  font-size: 0.75rem;
   361→  color: #92400e;
   362→  background: #fef3c7;
   363→  border: 1px solid #fcd34d;
   364→  border-radius: 4px;
   365→  padding: 0.3rem 0.6rem;
   366→  max-width: 360px;
   367→}
   368→
   369→/* ── Print ── */
   370→@media print {
   371→  .no-print {
   372→    display: none !important;
   373→  }
   374→
   375→  .app-container {
   376→    display: block;
   377→    height: auto;
   378→    overflow: visible;
   379→  }
   380→
   381→  .dual-panel-layout {
   382→    display: block;
   383→    min-width: unset;
   384→  }
   385→
   386→  .preview-panel {
   387→    background: transparent;
   388→    overflow: visible;
   389→  }
   390→}
   391→
   392→@media (max-width: 1024px) {
   393→  .dual-panel-layout {
   394→    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
   395→    grid-template-rows: 1fr 1fr;
   396→    min-width: unset;
   397→  }
   398→
   399→  .editor-panel {
   400→    border-right: none;
   401→    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-gray-300);
   402→  }
   403→}
   404→</style>
   405→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192<template>\n     2\u2192  <div class=\"app-container\">\n     3\u2192    <AppHeader class=\"app-header no-print\">\n     4\u2192      <template #right-actions>\n     5\u2192        <div class=\"export-area no-print\">\n     6\u2192          <div v-if=\"showDivergenceWarning\" class=\"divergence-warning\">\n     7\u2192            \u26a0 Some content may have been changed during formatting. Please review carefully before exporting.\n     8\u2192          </div>\n     9\u2192          <UButton\n    10\u2192            size=\"xs\"\n    11\u2192            variant=\"outline\"\n    12\u2192            icon=\"i-heroicons-printer\"\n    13\u2192            :disabled=\"!formattedXml\"\n    14\u2192            @click=\"handlePrint\"\n    15\u2192          >\n    16\u2192            Export PDF\n    17\u2192          </UButton>\n    18\u2192          <span class=\"export-hint\">Works best in Chrome. In the print dialog, choose 'Save as PDF'.</span>\n    19\u2192        </div>\n    20\u2192      </template>\n    21\u2192    </AppHeader>\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192    <!-- Dual-panel layout: Text input (left) and Preview (right) -->\n    24\u2192    <div class=\"dual-panel-layout\">\n    25\u2192      <!-- Left Panel: Plain text input -->\n    26\u2192      <div class=\"editor-panel no-print\">\n    27\u2192        <textarea\n    28\u2192          v-model=\"textContent\"\n    29\u2192          class=\"text-input\"\n    30\u2192          :placeholder=\"placeholder\"\n    31\u2192          :disabled=\"isFormatting\"\n    32\u2192        />\n    33\u2192        <div class=\"action-bar\">\n    34\u2192          <div class=\"action-row\">\n    35\u2192            <UButton\n    36\u2192              :disabled=\"!textContent.trim() || isFormatting\"\n    37\u2192              :loading=\"isFormatting\"\n    38\u2192              @click=\"formatDocument\"\n    39\u2192            >\n    40\u2192              {{ isFormatting ? 'Formatting...' : 'Format My Resume' }}\n    41\u2192            </UButton>\n    42\u2192            <p v-if=\"validationMessage\" class=\"validation-message\">\n    43\u2192              {{ validationMessage }}\n    44\u2192            </p>\n    45\u2192          </div>\n    46\u2192        </div>\n    47\u2192      </div>\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192      <!-- Right Panel: Preview -->\n    50\u2192      <div class=\"preview-panel\">\n    51\u2192        <!-- Skeleton loading state -->\n    52\u2192        <div v-if=\"isFormatting\" class=\"skeleton-wrapper no-print\">\n    53\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-name\" />\n    54\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-contact\" />\n    55\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton-divider\" />\n    56\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line\" />\n    57\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short\" />\n    58\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line\" />\n    59\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton-gap\" />\n    60\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-heading\" />\n    61\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line\" />\n    62\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--medium\" />\n    63\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line\" />\n    64\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short\" />\n    65\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton-gap\" />\n    66\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-heading\" />\n    67\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--medium\" />\n    68\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short\" />\n    69\u2192        </div>\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192        <!-- Error state -->\n    72\u2192        <div v-else-if=\"formatError\" class=\"error-state no-print\">\n    73\u2192          <p class=\"error-message\">{{ formatError }}</p>\n    74\u2192          <UButton variant=\"outline\" @click=\"formatDocument\">\n    75\u2192            Try Again\n    76\u2192          </UButton>\n    77\u2192        </div>\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192        <!-- Empty state -->\n    80\u2192        <div v-else-if=\"!formattedXml\" class=\"preview-empty no-print\">\n    81\u2192          <p>Your formatted document will appear here.</p>\n    82\u2192        </div>\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192        <!-- Preview + review note -->\n    85\u2192        <template v-else>\n    86\u2192          <PreviewPanel\n    87\u2192            :xml-content=\"formattedXml\"\n    88\u2192            :zoom=\"1\"\n    89\u2192          />\n    90\u2192          <div class=\"review-note no-print\">\n    91\u2192            Your content has been formatted but not rewritten. Please review before exporting.\n    92\u2192          </div>\n    93\u2192        </template>\n    94\u2192      </div>\n    95\u2192    </div>\n    96\u2192  </div>\n    97\u2192</template>\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192<script setup lang=\"ts\">\n   100\u2192import { ref, computed } from 'vue'\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192useHead({\n   103\u2192  title: 'OhMyDoc',\n   104\u2192})\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192const placeholder = `Paste your resume or cover letter here.\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192Example:\n   109\u2192Jane Smith\n   110\u2192jane@email.com \u00b7 Toronto, ON\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192Software Engineer with 8 years...`\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192const textContent = ref('')\n   115\u2192const formattedXml = ref('')\n   116\u2192const isFormatting = ref(false)\n   117\u2192const formatError = ref('')\n   118\u2192const documentType = ref('')\n   119\u2192const divergence = ref(0)\n   120\u2192const validationMessage = ref('')\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192const showDivergenceWarning = computed(() => formattedXml.value && divergence.value > 0.05)\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192function handlePrint() {\n   125\u2192  window.print()\n   126\u2192}\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192async function formatDocument() {\n   129\u2192  validationMessage.value = ''\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  if (!textContent.value.trim()) return\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192  if (textContent.value.trim().length < 50) {\n   134\u2192    validationMessage.value = 'Please paste at least 50 characters of content to format.'\n   135\u2192    return\n   136\u2192  }\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192  if (isFormatting.value) return\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  isFormatting.value = true\n   141\u2192  formatError.value = ''\n   142\u2192  formattedXml.value = ''\n   143\u2192  documentType.value = ''\n   144\u2192  divergence.value = 0\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192  try {\n   147\u2192    const result = await $fetch<{ xml: string, divergence: number, documentType: string }>('/api/format', {\n   148\u2192      method: 'POST',\n   149\u2192      body: { text: textContent.value },\n   150\u2192    })\n   151\u2192    formattedXml.value = result.xml\n   152\u2192    documentType.value = result.documentType\n   153\u2192    divergence.value = result.divergence\n   154\u2192  }\n   155\u2192  catch (err: unknown) {\n   156\u2192    const raw = (err as { data?: { rawResponse?: string } })?.data?.rawResponse\n   157\u2192    if (raw) {\n   158\u2192      console.error('[OhMyDoc] Raw AI response (malformed XML):', raw)\n   159\u2192    }\n   160\u2192    const message = (err as { data?: { message?: string } })?.data?.message\n   161\u2192    formatError.value = message || 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'\n   162\u2192  }\n   163\u2192  finally {\n   164\u2192    isFormatting.value = false\n   165\u2192  }\n   166\u2192}\n   167\u2192</script>\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192<style scoped>\n   170\u2192.app-container {\n   171\u2192  display: flex;\n   172\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   173\u2192  height: 100vh;\n   174\u2192  width: 100vw;\n   175\u2192  overflow: hidden;\n   176\u2192}\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192.dual-panel-layout {\n   179\u2192  display: grid;\n   180\u2192  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;\n   181\u2192  flex: 1;\n   182\u2192  min-height: 0;\n   183\u2192  gap: 0;\n   184\u2192  min-width: 1024px;\n   185\u2192}\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192.editor-panel {\n   188\u2192  display: flex;\n   189\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   190\u2192  overflow: hidden;\n   191\u2192  border-right: 1px solid var(--color-gray-200);\n   192\u2192}\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192.text-input {\n   195\u2192  flex: 1;\n   196\u2192  resize: none;\n   197\u2192  border: none;\n   198\u2192  outline: none;\n   199\u2192  padding: 1.25rem;\n   200\u2192  font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace;\n   201\u2192  font-size: 0.875rem;\n   202\u2192  line-height: 1.6;\n   203\u2192  color: var(--color-gray-900);\n   204\u2192  background-color: #fff;\n   205\u2192  min-height: 0;\n   206\u2192}\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192.text-input:disabled {\n   209\u2192  opacity: 0.6;\n   210\u2192  cursor: not-allowed;\n   211\u2192}\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192.action-bar {\n   214\u2192  padding: 0.75rem 1.25rem;\n   215\u2192  border-top: 1px solid var(--color-gray-200);\n   216\u2192  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);\n   217\u2192}\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192.action-row {\n   220\u2192  display: flex;\n   221\u2192  align-items: center;\n   222\u2192  gap: 0.75rem;\n   223\u2192  flex-wrap: wrap;\n   224\u2192}\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192.validation-message {\n   227\u2192  font-size: 0.8rem;\n   228\u2192  color: var(--color-amber-600, #d97706);\n   229\u2192}\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192.preview-panel {\n   232\u2192  display: flex;\n   233\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   234\u2192  overflow: auto;\n   235\u2192  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);\n   236\u2192  position: relative;\n   237\u2192}\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192.preview-empty {\n   240\u2192  flex: 1;\n   241\u2192  display: flex;\n   242\u2192  align-items: center;\n   243\u2192  justify-content: center;\n   244\u2192  color: var(--color-gray-400);\n   245\u2192  font-size: 0.875rem;\n   246\u2192}\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192/* \u2500\u2500 Skeleton loading UI \u2500\u2500 */\n   249\u2192.skeleton-wrapper {\n   250\u2192  padding: 2rem 1.5rem;\n   251\u2192  display: flex;\n   252\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   253\u2192  gap: 0.5rem;\n   254\u2192}\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192.skeleton {\n   257\u2192  background: linear-gradient(\n   258\u2192    90deg,\n   259\u2192    var(--color-gray-200) 25%,\n   260\u2192    var(--color-gray-100) 50%,\n   261\u2192    var(--color-gray-200) 75%\n   262\u2192  );\n   263\u2192  background-size: 200% 100%;\n   264\u2192  animation: shimmer 1.4s ease-in-out infinite;\n   265\u2192  border-radius: 4px;\n   266\u2192}\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192@keyframes shimmer {\n   269\u2192  0% { background-position: 200% 0; }\n   270\u2192  100% { background-position: -200% 0; }\n   271\u2192}\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192.skeleton-name {\n   274\u2192  height: 2rem;\n   275\u2192  width: 55%;\n   276\u2192  margin-bottom: 0.25rem;\n   277\u2192}\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192.skeleton-contact {\n   280\u2192  height: 1rem;\n   281\u2192  width: 70%;\n   282\u2192  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;\n   283\u2192}\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192.skeleton-divider {\n   286\u2192  height: 2px;\n   287\u2192  background: var(--color-gray-200);\n   288\u2192  margin: 0.75rem 0;\n   289\u2192  border-radius: 0;\n   290\u2192}\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192.skeleton-line {\n   293\u2192  height: 0.875rem;\n   294\u2192  width: 100%;\n   295\u2192}\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192.skeleton-line--short {\n   298\u2192  width: 55%;\n   299\u2192}\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192.skeleton-line--medium {\n   302\u2192  width: 75%;\n   303\u2192}\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192.skeleton-gap {\n   306\u2192  height: 1rem;\n   307\u2192}\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192.skeleton-heading {\n   310\u2192  height: 1rem;\n   311\u2192  width: 30%;\n   312\u2192  margin-bottom: 0.25rem;\n   313\u2192}\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192/* \u2500\u2500 Error state \u2500\u2500 */\n   316\u2192.error-state {\n   317\u2192  flex: 1;\n   318\u2192  display: flex;\n   319\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   320\u2192  align-items: center;\n   321\u2192  justify-content: center;\n   322\u2192  gap: 1rem;\n   323\u2192  padding: 2rem;\n   324\u2192}\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192.error-message {\n   327\u2192  color: var(--color-red-600);\n   328\u2192  font-size: 0.9rem;\n   329\u2192  text-align: center;\n   330\u2192  max-width: 320px;\n   331\u2192}\n   332\u2192\n   333\u2192/* \u2500\u2500 Review / mutation note \u2500\u2500 */\n   334\u2192.review-note {\n   335\u2192  padding: 0.6rem 1.25rem;\n   336\u2192  font-size: 0.75rem;\n   337\u2192  color: var(--color-gray-500);\n   338\u2192  background: var(--color-gray-50);\n   339\u2192  border-top: 1px solid var(--color-gray-200);\n   340\u2192  text-align: center;\n   341\u2192}\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192/* \u2500\u2500 Export area in header \u2500\u2500 */\n   344\u2192.export-area {\n   345\u2192  display: flex;\n   346\u2192  align-items: center;\n   347\u2192  gap: 0.5rem;\n   348\u2192  flex-wrap: wrap;\n   349\u2192  justify-content: flex-end;\n   350\u2192}\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192.export-hint {\n   353\u2192  font-size: 0.7rem;\n   354\u2192  color: var(--color-gray-400);\n   355\u2192  white-space: nowrap;\n   356\u2192}\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192/* \u2500\u2500 Divergence / mutation warning \u2500\u2500 */\n   359\u2192.divergence-warning {\n   360\u2192  font-size: 0.75rem;\n   361\u2192  color: #92400e;\n   362\u2192  background: #fef3c7;\n   363\u2192  border: 1px solid #fcd34d;\n   364\u2192  border-radius: 4px;\n   365\u2192  padding: 0.3rem 0.6rem;\n   366\u2192  max-width: 360px;\n   367\u2192}\n   368\u2192\n   369\u2192/* \u2500\u2500 Print \u2500\u2500 */\n   370\u2192@media print {\n   371\u2192  .no-print {\n   372\u2192    display: none !important;\n   373\u2192  }\n   374\u2192\n   375\u2192  .app-container {\n   376\u2192    display: block;\n   377\u2192    height: auto;\n   378\u2192    overflow: visible;\n   379\u2192  }\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192  .dual-panel-layout {\n   382\u2192    display: block;\n   383\u2192    min-width: unset;\n   384\u2192  }\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192  .preview-panel {\n   387\u2192    background: transparent;\n   388\u2192    overflow: visible;\n   389\u2192  }\n   390\u2192}\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192@media (max-width: 1024px) {\n   393\u2192  .dual-panel-layout {\n   394\u2192    grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n   395\u2192    grid-template-rows: 1fr 1fr;\n   396\u2192    min-width: unset;\n   397\u2192  }\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192  .editor-panel {\n   400\u2192    border-right: none;\n   401\u2192    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-gray-300);\n   402\u2192  }\n   403\u2192}\n   404\u2192</style>\n   405\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #38
OK, so the app has a different design than what's currently deployed. Looking at the Vercel deployment, it shows the old XML editor with Monaco editor. The `index.vue` in the codebase is the new version with: - Plain textarea for input - Format button - Skeleton loading - Preview panel - PDF export - Divergence warning But the deployed version seems to be the old version. Wait, let me re-check the deployed URL - maybe it redirected or is showing different content. Let me look again at the screenshot. Actually, looking at the screenshot more carefully: - The left panel has what appears to be a Monaco editor with XML - The right panel shows the rendered preview But in `index.vue`, the left panel should be a plain textarea. So the deployed version might actually be an older build. The user mentioned the deployment is at `https://ohmydoc-using-claude-code-agent.vercel.app` - I'm checking the git history to see if there were recent reverts that might explain why the deployed version doesn't match the current codebase. Looking at the recent commits, there was a revert of the Cloudflare Pages deployment configuration, which means the Vercel deployment should have the latest features. But when I examine the page structure in the screenshots, I'm seeing what looks like a Monaco editor setup rather than the plain textarea that's currently in the code, which suggests there's a mismatch between what's deployed and what's in the repository. The page title is also showing the old "OhMyDoc - XML to HTML Transformer" instead of just "OhMyDoc" that the current code sets, confirming the deployed version is outdated. I should do a hard refresh to see if that helps pull the latest build, and I need to clarify what the user actually wants me to test against. I'm realizing the deployed version might actually be the new interface, and I was misreading the screenshot—let me just write the tests against the live URL as requested and see which ones pass when I run them, since the test scenarios describe the AI-powered features that should be there. I'm realizing the deployed version might be showing an older iteration of the app than what's in the current codebase. The screenshot displays a Monaco editor with XML, but the code shows a textarea with a Format button instead. I should write tests based on the described scenarios and run them against the live URL to see what actually works. I need to write Playwright tests for the new app behavior as described in the scenarios, then run them against the live URL and report which ones pass or fail. Let me check what test commands are available in the package.json.

No .env file. I need to check if the Playwright tests use a `DEPLOYED_URL` env var or if I can set one. Looking at `playwright.e2e.config.ts`, it reads `process.env.DEPLOYED_URL`. I'll need to create the tests and run them with the URL. Now let me look at the existing e2e test for reference to understand the test structure used. The deployed URL is: `https://ohmydoc-using-claude-code-agent.vercel.app` Looking at the screenshot, the current deployed version seems to be the OLD XML editor. But the user wants me to write tests for the new AI-powered version. The deployed Vercel app is running an older version than what's in the current codebase—the live site shows the Monaco editor with XML preview, while the local `index.vue` has the new plain textarea with AI formatting. This mismatch is probably because Vercel hasn't deployed the recent commits yet. Looking at the git history, the most recent commit reverted the Cloudflare Pages configuration, but the actual feature code from the AI pipeline and Phase 2 updates should still be intact in the codebase. The deployed Vercel app might be auto-deploying from main and should have those new features, but the screenshot I'm seeing shows the old Monaco editor interface. Let me reload and examine the page elements more carefully to see if this is actually the updated version or if there's a caching issue. I need to clarify the task: write Playwright tests for the new AI-powered interface, run them against the deployed URL, and report which ones pass or fail. The deployed version might be outdated, which could explain test failures, but that's exactly what this QA exercise is meant to surface. Let me check if there's a `vercel.json` configuration in the project to understand the deployment setup.

USERMESSAGE #50
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→import { Parser } from 'htmlparser2'
     2→
     3→const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a professional document formatter. Convert plain-text resumes and cover letters into structured XML.
     4→
     5→For resumes use this schema:
     6→<document type="resume">
     7→  <header>
     8→    <name>Full Name</name>
     9→    <contact>
    10→      <email>email@example.com</email>
    11→      <phone>555-000-0000</phone>
    12→      <location>City, State</location>
    13→      <linkedin>linkedin.com/in/username</linkedin>
    14→      <website>portfolio.com</website>
    15→    </contact>
    16→  </header>
    17→  <summary>Professional summary text.</summary>
    18→  <experience>
    19→    <role>
    20→      <title>Job Title</title>
    21→      <company>Company Name</company>
    22→      <period>Month Year – Month Year</period>
    23→      <bullets>
    24→        <bullet>Key achievement or responsibility</bullet>
    25→      </bullets>
    26→    </role>
    27→  </experience>
    28→  <education>
    29→    <degree>Degree Name</degree>
    30→    <school>School Name</school>
    31→    <year>Graduation Year</year>
    32→    <note>Honors or relevant note</note>
    33→  </education>
    34→  <skills>Skill 1, Skill 2, Skill 3</skills>
    35→  <certifications>Cert 1, Cert 2</certifications>
    36→  <projects>Project descriptions</projects>
    37→  <volunteer>Volunteer experience</volunteer>
    38→</document>
    39→
    40→For cover letters use type="cover-letter" and adapt content into letter sections using summary (opening), experience (body paragraphs), and closing elements.
    41→
    42→Rules:
    43→- Preserve ALL information from the source text
    44→- Omit empty optional tags
    45→- Output ONLY valid XML — no markdown fences, no explanation
    46→- Detect whether the input is a resume or cover letter and set the type attribute accordingly`
    47→
    48→interface OpenAIChatResponse {
    49→  choices: Array<{
    50→    message: {
    51→      content: string
    52→    }
    53→  }>
    54→}
    55→
    56→function extractTextFromXml(xmlString: string): string {
    57→  const textParts: string[] = []
    58→  const parser = new Parser({
    59→    ontext(text) {
    60→      textParts.push(text)
    61→    },
    62→  }, { decodeEntities: true })
    63→  parser.write(xmlString)
    64→  parser.end()
    65→  return textParts.join(' ')
    66→}
    67→
    68→function detectDocumentType(xmlString: string): string {
    69→  const match = xmlString.match(/<document[^>]+type="([^"]+)"/)
    70→  return match?.[1] ?? 'resume'
    71→}
    72→
    73→function validateXmlStructure(xmlString: string): boolean {
    74→  let valid = true
    75→  let depth = 0
    76→  const parser = new Parser({
    77→    onerror() {
    78→      valid = false
    79→    },
    80→    onopentag() {
    81→      depth++
    82→    },
    83→    onclosetag() {
    84→      depth--
    85→      if (depth < 0) valid = false
    86→    },
    87→  }, { xmlMode: true })
    88→  parser.write(xmlString)
    89→  parser.end()
    90→  return valid && depth === 0
    91→}
    92→
    93→export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
    94→  const body = await readBody(event)
    95→
    96→  if (!body.text || body.text.length < 50) {
    97→    throw createError({ statusCode: 400, message: 'Paste more content to format.' })
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  const config = useRuntimeConfig()
   101→
   102→  if (!config.openaiApiKey) {
   103→    throw createError({ statusCode: 500, message: 'OpenAI API key is not configured.' })
   104→  }
   105→
   106→  const inputLen = body.text.length
   107→
   108→  const response = await $fetch<OpenAIChatResponse>('https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions', {
   109→    method: 'POST',
   110→    headers: {
   111→      'Authorization': `Bearer ${config.openaiApiKey}`,
   112→      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
   113→    },
   114→    body: {
   115→      model: 'gpt-4o-mini',
   116→      messages: [
   117→        { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT },
   118→        { role: 'user', content: body.text },
   119→      ],
   120→      temperature: 0.3,
   121→      max_tokens: 4000,
   122→    },
   123→  })
   124→
   125→  const raw = response.choices[0]?.message?.content ?? ''
   126→
   127→  // Strip markdown fences if present
   128→  const xml = raw
   129→    .replace(/^```(?:xml)?
?/m, '')
   130→    .replace(/
?```$/m, '')
   131→    .trim()
   132→
   133→  if (!validateXmlStructure(xml)) {
   134→    throw createError({ statusCode: 502, message: 'AI returned malformed XML. Please try again.' })
   135→  }
   136→
   137→  const xmlText = extractTextFromXml(xml)
   138→  const xmlTextLen = xmlText.length
   139→  const divergence = Math.abs(inputLen - xmlTextLen) / inputLen
   140→
   141→  const documentType = detectDocumentType(xml)
   142→
   143→  return { xml, divergence, documentType }
   144→})
   145→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

    "content": "     1\u2192import { Parser } from 'htmlparser2'\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a professional document formatter. Convert plain-text resumes and cover letters into structured XML.\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192For resumes use this schema:\n     6\u2192<document type=\"resume\">\n     7\u2192  <header>\n     8\u2192    <name>Full Name</name>\n     9\u2192    <contact>\n    10\u2192      <email>email@example.com</email>\n    11\u2192      <phone>555-000-0000</phone>\n    12\u2192      <location>City, State</location>\n    13\u2192      <linkedin>linkedin.com/in/username</linkedin>\n    14\u2192      <website>portfolio.com</website>\n    15\u2192    </contact>\n    16\u2192  </header>\n    17\u2192  <summary>Professional summary text.</summary>\n    18\u2192  <experience>\n    19\u2192    <role>\n    20\u2192      <title>Job Title</title>\n    21\u2192      <company>Company Name</company>\n    22\u2192      <period>Month Year \u2013 Month Year</period>\n    23\u2192      <bullets>\n    24\u2192        <bullet>Key achievement or responsibility</bullet>\n    25\u2192      </bullets>\n    26\u2192    </role>\n    27\u2192  </experience>\n    28\u2192  <education>\n    29\u2192    <degree>Degree Name</degree>\n    30\u2192    <school>School Name</school>\n    31\u2192    <year>Graduation Year</year>\n    32\u2192    <note>Honors or relevant note</note>\n    33\u2192  </education>\n    34\u2192  <skills>Skill 1, Skill 2, Skill 3</skills>\n    35\u2192  <certifications>Cert 1, Cert 2</certifications>\n    36\u2192  <projects>Project descriptions</projects>\n    37\u2192  <volunteer>Volunteer experience</volunteer>\n    38\u2192</document>\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192For cover letters use type=\"cover-letter\" and adapt content into letter sections using summary (opening), experience (body paragraphs), and closing elements.\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192Rules:\n    43\u2192- Preserve ALL information from the source text\n    44\u2192- Omit empty optional tags\n    45\u2192- Output ONLY valid XML \u2014 no markdown fences, no explanation\n    46\u2192- Detect whether the input is a resume or cover letter and set the type attribute accordingly`\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192interface OpenAIChatResponse {\n    49\u2192  choices: Array<{\n    50\u2192    message: {\n    51\u2192      content: string\n    52\u2192    }\n    53\u2192  }>\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192function extractTextFromXml(xmlString: string): string {\n    57\u2192  const textParts: string[] = []\n    58\u2192  const parser = new Parser({\n    59\u2192    ontext(text) {\n    60\u2192      textParts.push(text)\n    61\u2192    },\n    62\u2192  }, { decodeEntities: true })\n    63\u2192  parser.write(xmlString)\n    64\u2192  parser.end()\n    65\u2192  return textParts.join(' ')\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192function detectDocumentType(xmlString: string): string {\n    69\u2192  const match = xmlString.match(/<document[^>]+type=\"([^\"]+)\"/)\n    70\u2192  return match?.[1] ?? 'resume'\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192function validateXmlStructure(xmlString: string): boolean {\n    74\u2192  let valid = true\n    75\u2192  let depth = 0\n    76\u2192  const parser = new Parser({\n    77\u2192    onerror() {\n    78\u2192      valid = false\n    79\u2192    },\n    80\u2192    onopentag() {\n    81\u2192      depth++\n    82\u2192    },\n    83\u2192    onclosetag() {\n    84\u2192      depth--\n    85\u2192      if (depth < 0) valid = false\n    86\u2192    },\n    87\u2192  }, { xmlMode: true })\n    88\u2192  parser.write(xmlString)\n    89\u2192  parser.end()\n    90\u2192  return valid && depth === 0\n    91\u2192}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {\n    94\u2192  const body = await readBody(event)\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192  if (!body.text || body.text.length < 50) {\n    97\u2192    throw createError({ statusCode: 400, message: 'Paste more content to format.' })\n    98\u2192  }\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  const config = useRuntimeConfig()\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  if (!config.openaiApiKey) {\n   103\u2192    throw createError({ statusCode: 500, message: 'OpenAI API key is not configured.' })\n   104\u2192  }\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192  const inputLen = body.text.length\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192  const response = await $fetch<OpenAIChatResponse>('https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions', {\n   109\u2192    method: 'POST',\n   110\u2192    headers: {\n   111\u2192      'Authorization': `Bearer ${config.openaiApiKey}`,\n   112\u2192      'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n   113\u2192    },\n   114\u2192    body: {\n   115\u2192      model: 'gpt-4o-mini',\n   116\u2192      messages: [\n   117\u2192        { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT },\n   118\u2192        { role: 'user', content: body.text },\n   119\u2192      ],\n   120\u2192      temperature: 0.3,\n   121\u2192      max_tokens: 4000,\n   122\u2192    },\n   123\u2192  })\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192  const raw = response.choices[0]?.message?.content ?? ''\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  // Strip markdown fences if present\n   128\u2192  const xml = raw\n   129\u2192    .replace(/^```(?:xml)?\\n?/m, '')\n   130\u2192    .replace(/\\n?```$/m, '')\n   131\u2192    .trim()\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192  if (!validateXmlStructure(xml)) {\n   134\u2192    throw createError({ statusCode: 502, message: 'AI returned malformed XML. Please try again.' })\n   135\u2192  }\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  const xmlText = extractTextFromXml(xml)\n   138\u2192  const xmlTextLen = xmlText.length\n   139\u2192  const divergence = Math.abs(inputLen - xmlTextLen) / inputLen\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192  const documentType = detectDocumentType(xml)\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192  return { xml, divergence, documentType }\n   144\u2192})\n   145\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. ASSISTANTMESSAGE #51
Now I understand the full picture. The app has: 1. A plain textarea for raw text input 2. A "Format My Resume" button 3. AI API endpoint that returns structured XML 4. Preview panel with skeleton loading 5. Divergence warning 6. PDF export 7. Error states But the currently deployed Vercel version seems to show an older version with Monaco editor. Let me check by looking at this more carefully. Wait - looking at the test scenarios again: > ### 1. Basic Load > - Page loads without console errors > - Textarea is visible and accepts input > - Format button exists This confirms the new app has a textarea and Format button. There's a discrepancy between what I'm seeing on the deployed URL and what the test scenarios describe. Rather than get stuck on that, I should write the tests for the expected behavior and run them to see what's actually deployed. Looking at the git history, the latest main commit kept all the features despite reverting some configuration changes, so the deployment should be current. The page title shows the old "OhMyDoc - XML to HTML Transformer" text, but examining the accessibility snapshot more closely, I see that's the document title in the browser tab, while the actual heading on the page says "OhMyDoc v2". Looking at the code, the `useHead` configuration is setting just "OhMyDoc" as the title, which suggests there's a mismatch between what's deployed and what the current code should produce. This looks like a QA exercise where I need to write tests against the new expected behavior and run them on the deployed URL to see what passes and fails. I'm setting up a test file that will target the Vercel deployment and validate the new AI-powered interface. Writing test file... I'm setting up template strings with resume and cover letter data, including contact information, work history, and qualifications for both candidates. I need to set appropriate timeouts for the AI formatting tests since they'll take time to process. Looking at the component, I notice the `documentType` ref is defined but never rendered in the template, so that badge test might not be applicable to this version. Let me check what the PreviewPanel component actually displays...

USERMESSAGE #58
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→<template>
     2→  <div class="preview-panel">
     3→    <!-- Error Display -->
     4→    <UAlert
     5→      v-if="error"
     6→      color="red"
     7→      variant="solid"
     8→      title="Rendering Error"
     9→      :description="error"
    10→      icon="i-heroicons-exclamation-triangle"
    11→      class="error-alert"
    12→    />
    13→
    14→    <!-- Document Preview — content is DOMPurify-sanitized before assignment to sanitizedHtml -->
    15→    <!-- eslint-disable vue/no-v-html -->
    16→    <div
    17→      v-else-if="sanitizedHtml"
    18→      class="preview-container document-render"
    19→      :style="containerStyle"
    20→      v-html="sanitizedHtml"
    21→    />
    22→    <!-- eslint-enable vue/no-v-html -->
    23→
    24→    <!-- Placeholder State -->
    25→    <div v-else class="placeholder-state">
    26→      <p>Preparing preview…</p>
    27→    </div>
    28→  </div>
    29→</template>
    30→
    31→<script setup lang="ts">
    32→import { ref, computed, watch } from 'vue'
    33→import './resume-styles.css'
    34→
    35→defineOptions({ name: 'PreviewPanel' })
    36→
    37→interface Props {
    38→  xmlContent: string
    39→  zoom?: number
    40→}
    41→
    42→const props = withDefaults(defineProps<Props>(), { zoom: 1 })
    43→
    44→const sanitizedHtml = ref<string>('')
    45→const error = ref<string | undefined>(undefined)
    46→
    47→const containerStyle = computed(() => ({
    48→  transform: `scale(${props.zoom})`,
    49→  transformOrigin: 'top left',
    50→}))
    51→
    52→const ALLOWED_TAGS = [
    53→  'document', 'header', 'name', 'contact', 'email', 'phone', 'location',
    54→  'linkedin', 'website', 'summary', 'experience', 'role', 'title', 'company',
    55→  'period', 'bullets', 'bullet', 'education', 'degree', 'school', 'year',
    56→  'note', 'skills', 'certifications', 'projects', 'volunteer',
    57→  // Standard HTML tags DOMPurify allows anyway — kept for section labels
    58→  'section', 'h2', 'p', 'ul', 'li', 'a', 'br', 'strong', 'em',
    59→]
    60→
    61→const ALLOWED_ATTR = ['type', 'href']
    62→
    63→async function updatePreview() {
    64→  error.value = undefined
    65→  sanitizedHtml.value = ''
    66→
    67→  if (!props.xmlContent) return
    68→
    69→  try {
    70→    // DOMPurify is browser-only — import lazily to avoid SSR issues
    71→    const DOMPurify = (await import('dompurify')).default
    72→    const clean = DOMPurify.sanitize(props.xmlContent, {
    73→      ADD_TAGS: ALLOWED_TAGS,
    74→      ADD_ATTR: ALLOWED_ATTR,
    75→      FORCE_BODY: false,
    76→    })
    77→    sanitizedHtml.value = clean
    78→  }
    79→  catch (err) {
    80→    error.value = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Could not render document'
    81→  }
    82→}
    83→
    84→watch(() => props.xmlContent, updatePreview, { immediate: true })
    85→</script>
    86→
    87→<style scoped>
    88→.preview-panel {
    89→  width: 100%;
    90→  height: 100%;
    91→  overflow: auto;
    92→  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);
    93→  padding: 1.5rem 1.25rem;
    94→}
    95→
    96→.error-alert {
    97→  margin-bottom: 1rem;
    98→}
    99→
   100→.placeholder-state {
   101→  display: flex;
   102→  align-items: center;
   103→  justify-content: center;
   104→  min-height: 200px;
   105→  color: var(--color-gray-600);
   106→}
   107→
   108→.preview-container {
   109→  width: fit-content;
   110→  min-width: 100%;
   111→  padding-bottom: 2rem;
   112→}
   113→
   114→@media print {
   115→  .preview-panel {
   116→    overflow: visible;
   117→    height: auto;
   118→    background: transparent;
   119→    padding: 0;
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  .preview-container {
   123→    transform: none !important;
   124→    width: 100%;
   125→    padding-bottom: 0;
   126→  }
   127→}
   128→</style>
   129→

    "tool_use_id": "toolu_016tciQq7hWmn24Hsha6SVki",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192<template>\n     2\u2192  <div class=\"preview-panel\">\n     3\u2192    <!-- Error Display -->\n     4\u2192    <UAlert\n     5\u2192      v-if=\"error\"\n     6\u2192      color=\"red\"\n     7\u2192      variant=\"solid\"\n     8\u2192      title=\"Rendering Error\"\n     9\u2192      :description=\"error\"\n    10\u2192      icon=\"i-heroicons-exclamation-triangle\"\n    11\u2192      class=\"error-alert\"\n    12\u2192    />\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192    <!-- Document Preview \u2014 content is DOMPurify-sanitized before assignment to sanitizedHtml -->\n    15\u2192    <!-- eslint-disable vue/no-v-html -->\n    16\u2192    <div\n    17\u2192      v-else-if=\"sanitizedHtml\"\n    18\u2192      class=\"preview-container document-render\"\n    19\u2192      :style=\"containerStyle\"\n    20\u2192      v-html=\"sanitizedHtml\"\n    21\u2192    />\n    22\u2192    <!-- eslint-enable vue/no-v-html -->\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192    <!-- Placeholder State -->\n    25\u2192    <div v-else class=\"placeholder-state\">\n    26\u2192      <p>Preparing preview\u2026</p>\n    27\u2192    </div>\n    28\u2192  </div>\n    29\u2192</template>\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192<script setup lang=\"ts\">\n    32\u2192import { ref, computed, watch } from 'vue'\n    33\u2192import './resume-styles.css'\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192defineOptions({ name: 'PreviewPanel' })\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192interface Props {\n    38\u2192  xmlContent: string\n    39\u2192  zoom?: number\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192const props = withDefaults(defineProps<Props>(), { zoom: 1 })\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192const sanitizedHtml = ref<string>('')\n    45\u2192const error = ref<string | undefined>(undefined)\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192const containerStyle = computed(() => ({\n    48\u2192  transform: `scale(${props.zoom})`,\n    49\u2192  transformOrigin: 'top left',\n    50\u2192}))\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192const ALLOWED_TAGS = [\n    53\u2192  'document', 'header', 'name', 'contact', 'email', 'phone', 'location',\n    54\u2192  'linkedin', 'website', 'summary', 'experience', 'role', 'title', 'company',\n    55\u2192  'period', 'bullets', 'bullet', 'education', 'degree', 'school', 'year',\n    56\u2192  'note', 'skills', 'certifications', 'projects', 'volunteer',\n    57\u2192  // Standard HTML tags DOMPurify allows anyway \u2014 kept for section labels\n    58\u2192  'section', 'h2', 'p', 'ul', 'li', 'a', 'br', 'strong', 'em',\n    59\u2192]\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192const ALLOWED_ATTR = ['type', 'href']\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192async function updatePreview() {\n    64\u2192  error.value = undefined\n    65\u2192  sanitizedHtml.value = ''\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192  if (!props.xmlContent) return\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192  try {\n    70\u2192    // DOMPurify is browser-only \u2014 import lazily to avoid SSR issues\n    71\u2192    const DOMPurify = (await import('dompurify')).default\n    72\u2192    const clean = DOMPurify.sanitize(props.xmlContent, {\n    73\u2192      ADD_TAGS: ALLOWED_TAGS,\n    74\u2192      ADD_ATTR: ALLOWED_ATTR,\n    75\u2192      FORCE_BODY: false,\n    76\u2192    })\n    77\u2192    sanitizedHtml.value = clean\n    78\u2192  }\n    79\u2192  catch (err) {\n    80\u2192    error.value = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Could not render document'\n    81\u2192  }\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192watch(() => props.xmlContent, updatePreview, { immediate: true })\n    85\u2192</script>\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192<style scoped>\n    88\u2192.preview-panel {\n    89\u2192  width: 100%;\n    90\u2192  height: 100%;\n    91\u2192  overflow: auto;\n    92\u2192  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);\n    93\u2192  padding: 1.5rem 1.25rem;\n    94\u2192}\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192.error-alert {\n    97\u2192  margin-bottom: 1rem;\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192.placeholder-state {\n   101\u2192  display: flex;\n   102\u2192  align-items: center;\n   103\u2192  justify-content: center;\n   104\u2192  min-height: 200px;\n   105\u2192  color: var(--color-gray-600);\n   106\u2192}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192.preview-container {\n   109\u2192  width: fit-content;\n   110\u2192  min-width: 100%;\n   111\u2192  padding-bottom: 2rem;\n   112\u2192}\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192@media print {\n   115\u2192  .preview-panel {\n   116\u2192    overflow: visible;\n   117\u2192    height: auto;\n   118\u2192    background: transparent;\n   119\u2192    padding: 0;\n   120\u2192  }\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192  .preview-container {\n   123\u2192    transform: none !important;\n   124\u2192    width: 100%;\n   125\u2192    padding-bottom: 0;\n   126\u2192  }\n   127\u2192}\n ASSISTANTMESSAGE #59
Now I have a full picture of the app. Let me understand what I need to test:1. **Basic Load**: Page loads, textarea visible, Format button exists 2. **Happy Path — Resume**: Paste resume text, click Format, see skeleton, then preview renders 3. **Happy Path — Cover Letter**: Same for cover letter 4. **Mutation Warning**: The divergence warning appears when AI output diverges >5% 5. **PDF Export**: Click Export PDF button 6. **Error States**: Empty input, short input (< 50 chars) 7. **Skeleton Loading**: Skeleton visible during loading, disappears after Key elements to identify: - Textarea: `<textarea class="text-input">` - Format button: `<UButton>Format My Resume</UButton>` - Skeleton: `.skeleton-wrapper` visible during formatting - Error: `.error-message` with color-red-600 - Validation message: `.validation-message` - Preview panel: `.document-render` or `.preview-container` - Divergence warning: `.divergence-warning` - Export PDF button: "Export PDF" button in header - Review note: `.review-note` I'm identifying the key DOM elements I'll need to target for testing, then deciding whether to test against the current deployed version or wait for the updated one with the Monaco editor. Since the deployed version might be outdated, I'll focus my tests on the new version instead. I need to account for the fact that AI formatting operations will be slow—around 60 seconds—so I'll set generous timeouts and mark those tests as slow. The test configuration already expects a `DEPLOYED_URL` environment variable, so I'll structure my tests accordingly and create the spec file at `tests/e2e/ohmydoc-qa.spec.ts`. One thing I'm noticing: the `index.vue` template doesn't actually render the `documentType` property, but one of my test scenarios expects a document type badge to display. I need to investigate whether that's a missing feature or if I'm looking at the wrong part of the code.
